lesson.menu_title: At the Pharmacy -- Buying Medicine (Can I have...?)
lesson.menu_desc: Ask for medicine politely at the pharmacy: Can I have a painkiller, please? How much is it? New words: pharmacy, pharmacist, prescription, pill, painkiller, dose, allergy, label -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- At the Pharmacy: Buying Medicine (Can I have...?)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Érica Maria Machado Santarem | Aula 19 -- At the Pharmacy: Buying Medicine

listenings.text: Hello. Let me tell you about my morning. I had a headache, so I went to the pharmacy near my home. At the counter, I said: Can I have a painkiller, please? The pharmacist was very kind. She gave me a small box of pills. I asked: How much is it? She said: It is twelve reais. Then I asked one more question: I have an allergy. Is this medicine safe for me? She read the label and said: Yes, it is safe. Take one pill a day. Now I am at home. The painkiller helped, and my headache is gone.

Hello. This is the pharmacist. Let me give you some simple advice for the pharmacy. When you need medicine, ask politely: Can I have a painkiller, please? To know the price, ask: How much is it? Always read the label before you take the medicine. The label shows the dose: how many pills you take and when. If you have an allergy, tell the pharmacist first. For some medicine, you need a prescription from your doctor. Take care, and take the right dose.

extra_audio.text: First, I say: Hello. Can I have a painkiller, please? Second, the pharmacist asks: Do you have a prescription? Third, I answer: No, just a painkiller for a headache. Fourth, I ask: How much is it? Fifth, the pharmacist says: It is twelve reais. Read the label first.
